posts.title: Introduction to Client-Side Rendering
posts.body: Client-side rendering moves HTML generation from the server to the browser. The server sends a minimal HTML shell and JavaScript bundles, then React takes over and builds the UI entirely in the browser.

posts.title: Understanding the Virtual DOM
posts.body: React maintains a lightweight in-memory representation of the DOM called the virtual DOM. When state changes, React diffs the old and new virtual trees and applies only the minimal set of real DOM mutations needed.

posts.title: React Router and Single-Page Applications
posts.body: React Router intercepts browser navigation events and swaps page components without a full reload. createBrowserRouter and the Outlet component let you nest layouts while keeping the URL as the source of truth.

posts.title: Data Fetching with TanStack Query
posts.body: TanStack Query treats server data as a separate cache layer. useQuery automatically handles loading, error, and stale states, while useMutation plus invalidateQueries keeps the cache consistent after writes.

posts.title: Global State with Redux Toolkit
posts.body: Redux Toolkit reduces Redux boilerplate with createSlice and createAsyncThunk. The store holds client-owned global state, while server cache (fetched data) is better handled by React Query.

posts.title: Forms and Validation with React Hook Form
posts.body: React Hook Form avoids controlled components for performance. useForm registers fields via refs, Zod schemas define validation rules, and zodResolver bridges the two. Errors are type-safe and accessible.

posts.title: Animations with Framer Motion
posts.body: Framer Motion wraps standard HTML elements with motion.* variants. AnimatePresence handles mount/unmount transitions, layout animations automatically FLIP between positions, and useReducedMotion respects user preferences.

posts.title: Vite vs Webpack: Build Tool Comparison
posts.body: Vite uses native ES modules in development for near-instant HMR. Webpack bundles everything upfront with a module graph of loaders and plugins. Vite uses Rollup for production; Webpack uses its own optimiser. Both support code splitting.

posts.title: Code Splitting and Lazy Loading
posts.body: React.lazy and dynamic import() tell the bundler to split a component into a separate chunk loaded on demand. Wrapping lazy routes with Suspense lets you show a fallback spinner while the chunk downloads.

posts.title: Babel and JavaScript Transforms
posts.body: Babel transforms modern JavaScript (JSX, TypeScript, class fields) into syntax older environments understand. Presets like @babel/preset-env select transforms based on browserslist targets. Plugins handle one specific transform each.
